comments.body: If you are using a client ID, tenant ID, and client secret, you can use ClientSecretCredential to authenticate with Azure Storage

@Venkatesan Yes I have tried that. Using the same creds. No difference. It's not to do with the method of Auth, as they work fine with the CLI. There is some difference in how the java lib sends the request...
@EdwardRixon , Have you tried to login with Azure CLI before running the code or You login using Az login
@EdwardRixon stackoverflow.com/questions/79495357/… Check the ClientSecretCredential code and try it from your end.
@Venkatesan yo thanks for your help I found the mistake! answer incoming

answers.body:
Ok turns out I made a dumb mistake with the storage account name in the app config! So the error message is a bit misleading, but also makes sense. It was trying to connect to the wrong storage account, which does explain the error message.

I would advise anyone who has this same issue, add this logging option to your client:

val httpLogOptions = com.azure.core.http.policy.HttpLogOptions()
        .setLogLevel(HttpLogDetailLevel.BODY_AND_HEADERS)

    return BlobServiceClientBuilder()
        .endpoint(url)
        .credential(credential)
        .httpLogOptions(httpLogOptions)
        .addPolicy(HttpLoggingPolicy(httpLogOptions))
        .buildClient()

It allowed me to see the request and headers.

title: Issuer validation failed. Issuer did not match. Azure BlobContainerClient
body:
I am trying to upload to azure blob storage. I have successfully uploaded using the azure cli:

az login --service-principal -u <clientId> -p <clientSecret> --tenant <tenantId> && 
az storage blob upload \
  --container-name "\$web" \
  --account-name <accountName> \
  --name test.txt \
  --file test.txt \
  --auth-mode login

But when I attempt to upload with the same credentials from my application, using the BlobContainerClient like so:

    val defaultAzureCredential: DefaultAzureCredential =
        DefaultAzureCredentialBuilder()
            .tenantId(azureConfig.azureTenantId)
            .build()

    val blobServiceClient =
        blobServiceClientBuilder
            .endpoint(azureConfig.storageAccountUrl)
            .credential(defaultAzureCredential)
            .buildClient()
    val blobContainerClient = blobServiceClient.getBlobContainerClient(STORAGE_ACCOUNT_CONTAINER)
    try {
        val fileName = "${appSlug}_${LocalDateTime.now().format(pattern)}.zip"
        val blobClient = blobContainerClient.getBlobClient(fileName)

        blobClient.upload(inputStream, true)

I get the following error:

<AuthenticationErrorDetail>Issuer validation failed. Issuer did not match.</AuthenticationErrorDetail>

The issuer in the token in the cli is https://sts.windows.net/<tenantId>/ and I checked getting the token from the defaultAzureCredential, and it has the same issuer.

I really don't know why it's not working when using the java lib... Any ideas?
